title: Least Common Divisor
description:
A divisor of string $A$ is a string $D$ which can be repeated an integer number of times to obtain $A$. For example, divisors of string "aaaa" are strings "a", "aa", and "aaaa", and divisors of string "ababab" are strings "ab" and "ababab".

Consider two strings $S$ and $T$. Find the shortest of strings which are simultaneously divisors of $S$ and divisors of $T$, or determine that there are no such strings.
input: The first line contains string $S$, and the second line contains string $T$. Each of these strings has length from $1$ to $50$ characters, inclusive, and consists only of lowercase English letters.
output: Print the least common divisor of strings $S$ and $T$, or the string "No solution" in case the least common divisor does not exist.
source: Contest, Open Cup, 2018/2019 Season, Stage 17: Grand Prix of Baltic Sea
